Stakeholder Demo Hub The Agent Command Center - MVP-50 readiness review
Home Presentation System Story System Scale Validator Map Launch Live Dashboard

Read-only demo package

Stakeholder Demo Hub - The Agent Command Center

A premium browser-viewable demo hub for the production-visible readiness architecture. This package is verified through MVP-50, keeps runtime activation disabled, and turns the Markdown source package into a guided stakeholder experience.

Production verified through MVP-50 NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION Runtime activation not started Read-only static HTML
Launch Live Dashboard Open Presentation Mode
Readiness layers 8 MVP-43 through MVP-50
Production reports 67 Repo-derived verification reports
Validators 280 Python validator scripts
Agent count UNKNOWN UNKNOWN_NOT_CURRENTLY_DECLARED

Safety boundary

This is a viewable static demo package. It does not enable runtime behavior, command execution, action execution, writes, automation, or rollback.

Guided demo

What to open first

Use these pages for the stakeholder walk-through. Each page is static HTML and stays inside the Netlify publish directory.

Stakeholder Presentation

The 12-slide storyline, rendered as browser-readable sections for executive review and screen-share pacing.

Open

System Story

Plain-English explanation of the command center, why readiness matters, and why runtime remains disabled.

Open

System Scale

Repo-derived counts with honest labels for known and unknown operating-unit totals.

Open

Agent / Department Hierarchy

The seven-level hierarchy, the 11 operational domains, and the places where canonical registries still do not exist.

Open

Operating Model

The request flow from identity through audit, approval, dry-run, queueing, human review, and go / no-go.

Open

Validator and Safety Gate Map

The flat E2E validator pattern, the master wall, and the specific safety markers that keep runtime off.

Open

Safety Boundaries

A compact readout of the runtime capabilities that remain disabled.

Open

Technical Appendix

Validator strategy, flat E2E design, known limitations, and future runtime activation gates.

Open

Objections / FAQ

Twenty-plus stakeholder questions answered directly and without hype.

Open

Review / Scorecard

Executive questions, technical checks, safety criteria, and the demo success scorecard.

Open

System scale snapshot

Verified counts from the repo

Every exact count below comes from manifests, file counts, or dashboard data. Exact agent and department counts are not canonically declared.

Readiness layers

8 layers, verified through MVP-50.

Production verification reports

67 repo-derived reports.

Product-track files

582 files in `09_exports/mvp_product_track`.

Release artifacts

177 files in `09_exports/release_package`.

UI model JSON files

226 files in `14_backend/product_runtime/ui_models`.

Validator scripts

280 `validate_*.py` scripts in `scripts/`.

Dashboard static artifacts

70 files in `13_web_dashboard/dist`.

Stakeholder package files

19 files in the stakeholder presentation package.

Demo package files

16 files in the external demo package.

Viewable demo pages

11 HTML pages in this hub.

Operational domains

11 documented domains instead of a fake department registry.

Disabled capability categories

13 categories in the presentation manifest.

At a glance

What this hub says and does not say

What it says

  • Production verified through MVP-50.
  • Roadmap complete through MVP-50.
  • Runtime activation has not started.
  • Validators, safety gates, and review materials are visible.

What it does not say

  • No live automation.
  • No command execution.
  • No public writes, database writes, or Supabase writes.
  • No alert sending, rollback execution, incident mutation, or deploy controls.

Exact counts we refuse to fake

  • Exact agent count: UNKNOWN_NOT_CURRENTLY_DECLARED.
  • Exact department count: UNKNOWN_NOT_CURRENTLY_DECLARED.
  • Formal registries are recommended for the next docs pass.
This is a static browser demo hub. It exists to make the stakeholder package viewable inside the Netlify publish directory without enabling runtime behavior.
